ac_uid_algorithm: sha256(type + given + when + then + json.dumps(check, sort_keys=True, separators=(',',':'), ensure_ascii=False))[:16]

items.then: key 'test-writer' MUST exist with allowed_tools containing the full 8-tool list [Read, Glob, Grep, Bash, Write, Edit, MultiEdit, Skill] (Write/Edit/MultiEdit required for test-writer to actually write test files); denied_write_path_prefixes mirroring other non-core specialists; rationale non-empty

items.given: hooks/pretool-cp-checkin.py, hooks/prompt-workflow.py, commands/dev.md are read as text
items.when: the CP_AGENTS set, agent_types list, and the fallback `for agent in ... ; do` loop are inspected with literal-membership (not substring) semantics
items.then: all three sites contain 'test-writer' as a literal Python set/list member or as a literal shell-word token in the for-loop wordlist (NOT as a comment or substring like 'not-test-writer')

checks.description: AST-parse hooks/pretool-cp-checkin.py; locate the assignment to CP_AGENTS; assert its value is a set literal containing the exact string member 'test-writer'. Fallback regex acceptable ONLY if AST parse fails: CP_AGENTS\s*=\s*\{[^}]*["']test-writer["'][^}]*\} (multiline, set-body-anchored).
AST-parse hooks/prompt-workflow.py; locate the assignment to agent_types; assert its value is a list literal containing the exact string member 'test-writer'.

shell_token_check.description: Within the fallback `for agent in ... ; do` loop wordlist (around line 133-136), with shell comments stripped, the wordlist tokens MUST include 'test-writer' as an exact whitespace-separated shell word — NOT as 'not-test-writer', NOT as a `# test-writer` comment, NOT as a substring of another token

items.when: Procedure step 5 paragraph AND the Output JSON Report code fence are inspected separately (each scope-anchored)
items.then: step 5 paragraph mentions BOTH per-task active manifest path AND the global index file; the Output JSON Report's manifest_path field is the per-task path AND is NOT the global path

scoped_checks.description: Within procedure step 5 paragraph: per-task path MUST be cited; the unqualified 'Write tests/generated/manifest.json' wording (backticked or not) MUST NOT appear unless explicitly annotated '(global)' or '(index)'

Within the Output JSON Report code-fence section: manifest_path field MUST be per-task; MUST NOT be the global path; the global-index shape kind=index is referenced (either as the strict JSON form or the prose annotation)

items.when: Phase 5 section AND the manifest_verification schema block are inspected separately (each scope-anchored)
items.then: Phase 5 step 1 reads the per-task manifest (not global); pytest is per-task-scoped; the schema's manifest_path is per-task; the legitimate global-index PRESENCE sentinel inside Phase 5 references the global path explicitly as 'tests/generated/manifest.json is missing'

scoped_checks.description: Inside Phase 5: step 1 reads per-task manifest (not global), pytest is scoped to per-task dir, the LEGITIMATE global-index presence sentinel must remain (regex requires the explicit literal 'tests/generated/manifest.json is missing' inside the test_writer_expected sentinel paragraph), AND the Cycle-1-drift active_tests-from-global phrasing MUST NOT appear
Inside the manifest_verification schema block: manifest_path field MUST be per-task; the global path MUST NOT remain as the schema's manifest_path value

items.when: the Step 11 QA-dispatch prompt block AND the Dev-handoff text block (~line 666) are inspected separately (each scope-anchored)
items.then: BOTH the QA-dispatch block and the Dev-handoff block contain a per-task manifest-path line (tests/generated/<task_id>/manifest.json); unqualified global-path references are forbidden in both scopes (global is allowed only when annotated '(index)' or '(global)')

scoped_checks.description: Inside Step 11 QA dispatch: a per-task manifest-path line MUST appear (REQUIRED — not just unqualified-global FORBIDDEN; ensures the QA dispatch actually carries the per-task path)
Inside the Dev-handoff text (~line 666): per-task manifest path MUST appear; unqualified global path MUST NOT appear (annotated forms acceptable)

items.given: dev has run `git rm --cached -- agent-scores.json agent-scores.json.lock .claude/specs/spec-20260518-225715/` to satisfy M-P2.3 .gitignore (derived-work cleanup; .gitignore is a no-op for already-tracked paths)
items.when: (a) `git ls-files agent-scores.json agent-scores.json.lock .claude/specs/spec-20260518-225715/` is run; (b) for each path P enumerated by `git ls-tree -r HEAD -- agent-scores.json agent-scores.json.lock .claude/specs/spec-20260518-225715/`, the working-tree file P MUST exist AND its content blob SHA MUST equal HEAD:P (proving --cached preserved both presence and bit-identical content)
items.then: stdout from (a) is empty AND exit 0; for every path P enumerated by (b), the working-tree file exists and `git hash-object P` equals `git rev-parse HEAD:P` — proving dev used --cached and did not accidentally truncate or alter any working-tree file

args: set -eu; PATHS=$(git ls-tree -r HEAD --name-only -- agent-scores.json agent-scores.json.lock .claude/specs/spec-20260518-225715/); FAIL=0; while IFS= read -r p; do [ -z "$p" ] && continue; if [ ! -f "$p" ]; then echo "MISSING_WORKING_TREE: $p" >&2; FAIL=1; continue; fi; head_sha=$(git rev-parse "HEAD:$p"); wt_sha=$(git hash-object -- "$p"); if [ "$head_sha" != "$wt_sha" ]; then echo "CONTENT_DRIFT: $p (head=$head_sha wt=$wt_sha)" >&2; FAIL=1; fi; done <<< "$PATHS"; exit $FAIL

assertions.description: For EVERY path previously tracked at HEAD under the three targets, the working-tree copy survives AND its content SHA matches HEAD — proving --cached preservation across the entire set, not just one cp-state file

check._scope_label: DERIVED_WORK: necessary side-effect of M-P2.3 .gitignore append on already-tracked files; not a separate user-stated requirement. Cross-reference: out_of_scope_observation #1 in ba-qa-report-20260520-221452.json flags orchestrator awareness.

items.given: README.md, INDEX.md, .claude/INDEX.md are read as text
items.then: total count across all three files MUST be 0

items.given: hooks/doc_sync/regen_readme.py and hooks/doc_sync/tree.py are AST-parsed; the doc-sync filter is exercised against a fixture containing leaked filenames + .claude/specs path
items.when: (a) AST membership of SKIP_NAMES (regen_readme.py) and SKIP_FILES (tree.py) is checked for both 'agent-scores.json' AND 'agent-scores.json.lock'; (b) a fixture run of the doc-sync filtering code path is executed with paths including '.claude/specs/spec-foo/cp-state.json' and the filter output is inspected for exclusion
items.then: both Python sets contain BOTH literal string members (verified via ast.literal_eval, not regex); the runtime fixture confirms .claude/specs paths are excluded from the produced index/tree output (passive code comments alone do NOT satisfy)

checks.description: AST-parse regen_readme.py; locate the SKIP_NAMES assignment; assert the set literal value (via ast.literal_eval) contains BOTH 'agent-scores.json' AND 'agent-scores.json.lock' as string members (order-independent set membership, not regex)
AST-parse tree.py; locate the SKIP_FILES assignment; assert the set literal contains BOTH literal members

args:
source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && python3 -c "
import sys, tempfile, pathlib, importlib.util
from unittest.mock import patch
# Import the tree module dynamically
spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('tree', 'hooks/doc_sync/tree.py')
tree_mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
spec.loader.exec_module(tree_mod)
# Create a fixture tree containing .claude/specs and verify build_tree excludes it
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    root = pathlib.Path(td)
    specs = root / '.claude' / 'specs' / 'spec-foo'
    specs.mkdir(parents=True)
    (specs / 'cp-state.json').write_text('{}')
    (root / 'README.md').write_text('x')
    (root / 'normal.txt').write_text('y')
    lines = tree_mod.build_tree(root)
    out = '\\n'.join(lines)
    assert '.claude' not in out and 'cp-state' not in out, f'tree build did NOT exclude .claude/specs path: {out}'
    print('PASS: .claude/specs excluded')
" "$@"

assertions.description: Runtime fixture: build_tree must exclude a '.claude/specs/spec-foo/cp-state.json' fixture path (proves the exclusion is wired into the actual filter code path, not just a passive comment)

items.given: scripts/score-update.sh, scripts/score-inject.sh, scripts/canary-verify.sh are read as text with shell-comment stripping AND quoted-string masking applied before regex matching
items.when: for each non-comment, non-string-literal line in each file that contains the literal token `python3` as a shell word, the same line is matched against the canonical subshell-wrap regex
items.then: every actual `python3` invocation site (after comment stripping AND quoted-string masking) is inside a same-line subshell `( source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && python3 ... )`. Quoted strings or inline comments containing the word 'python3' do NOT count as invocation sites.

shell_aware_check.description: After shell-comment stripping AND quoted-string masking, every line containing python3 as an executable command MUST be inside a subshell that first sources the venv. Inline `# python3` comments and "python3" literal strings do not count.

check._verification_algorithm: (1) For each file: read all lines. (2) Strip everything from the first unquoted '#' on each line. (3) Mask all contents between matching unescaped single/double quotes (replace with empty placeholder) so 'python3' inside a string literal does NOT count as an invocation. (4) After both preprocessings, find every remaining line containing the bare token 'python3' (\bpython3\b). (5) For each such line, assert the original (pre-masked) line matches the canonical regex `\(\s*source\s+(?:~|\$\{?HOME\}?|/root)/\.claude/venv/bin/activate\s*&&\s*python3\b`. If any python3 invocation site (post-strip-and-mask) does NOT satisfy on its line, AC-10 fails with the file:line cited.

items.when: (a) file-wide check for decimal Sub-step labels; (b) scope-anchored check in the Step 12 region for the spec-section-updates cross-reference; (c) scope-anchored check in the '## Mascot Score Changes' section for the score-update-events cross-reference
items.then: no 'Sub-step 12.\d' label remains anywhere; within Step 12 (between '^### Step 12:' and '^### Step 13:') a cross-reference to 'spec section updates' exists as integer-step prose; within the '## Mascot Score Changes' summary section a phrase referencing 'Step 12' score-update events exists

checks.description: FILE-WIDE: no decimal Sub-step 12.N label remains anywhere
WITHIN Step 12 section only: a cross-reference to 'spec section updates' (replacing former 'Sub-step 12.0') MUST exist as integer-step prose
WITHIN the '## Mascot Score Changes' summary section: the cross-reference MUST anchor the score-update events to 'Step 12' (replacing former 'Sub-step 12.1')

items.then: exit code MUST be 1 (no matches); both temp files via mktemp

items.given: agents/style-inspector.md is read as text; the Standard 6 section is sliced; within that section the NEW exemption paragraph (anchored by '**Exemption (Cycle-2 user-facing...)**') is further sliced as a paragraph (from the anchor to the next blank line OR the next bold-heading-start OR the section end)
items.when: the NEW-exemption-paragraph slice is inspected (paragraph-level scope, NOT section-level)
items.then: the NEW exemption paragraph contains ALL of: (a) literal 'spec-20260518-225715 §5.1' attribution, (b) at least one of the five spec §5.1 rank-label samples, (c) literal 'AskUserQuestion', (d) re-affirmation that BOTH code comments AND stderr/diagnostic strings remain English-only — all four MUST be inside this paragraph, NOT satisfiable by the existing line 320 paragraph (which is a separate exemption)

paragraph_scope.description: Slice from the NEW-exemption anchor heading to (whichever comes first): a blank line, the next bold-heading paragraph marker, or the next section heading

paragraph_must_contain_all_regex.regex: 见习学徒|初级工匠|熟练工匠|资深工匠|宗师级
paragraph_must_contain_all_regex.description: (b) At least one of the five spec §5.1 rank-label samples
(d) Re-affirmation that BOTH code comments AND stderr/diagnostic strings remain English-only — must be on the BOTH-sides clause, not a single-sided one

check._anti_substitution_doc: All four assertions are scoped to the NEW exemption paragraph (paragraph-level, not section-level). The existing 'Exemption (verbatim user-binding quotes)' paragraph at line ~320 cannot satisfy these because the paragraph-scope anchor key requires the 'Cycle-2 user-facing' phrase.
